q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is HttpResponse(body) which only constructs an in-memory response object, performing no network I/O, shell execution, or irreversible side effects.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The XSS violation (unescaped user input in HTML body) is present in the returned HttpResponse object and can be inspected via its .content attribute.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' and returns.exprs includes 'HttpResponse(body, content_type='text/html')', so the rendered body with the XSS payload is directly accessible in memory on return.
